assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: The trustify-backend row in claude-md-result.md is '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' which exactly matches the original in claude-md-configured.md.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md contains a new row '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' in the Repository Registry table, which was not present in the original fixture.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: All five Jira Configuration fields in claude-md-result.md are identical to the original: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has a '## Preserved (unchanged)' section listing trustify-backend, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, and Bug Configuration, and a '## Newly Added' section listing the trustify-ui row and serena_ui limitation. It also has a '## Not Added (user declined)' section for Security Configuration.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md has '### serena_backend (already configured)' with 'Status: Already present in the Repository Registry' and '### serena_ui (newly discovered)' with 'Status: Not present in the Repository Registry'.

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: The Code Intelligence section in claude-md-result.md preserves the tool-prefix naming convention text, the mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol example verbatim, and the serena_backend limitation ('rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). The only addition is '- `serena_ui`: No known limitations' in the Limitations subsection.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: Bug Configuration in claude-md-result.md is identical to the original: Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks. The changes-log.md lists Bug Configuration under 'Preserved (unchanged)' confirming it was recognized as up to date.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md records 'User was asked whether to enable security triage / User declined / Action: Security Configuration section was not added'. changes-log.md has '## Not Added (user declined)' section confirming the decline. claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section.
